top_signals.title: Stocks Climb Late After Micron’s Blowout Outlook: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Stocks climbed in late hours on speculation that Micron Technology Inc.’s strong outlook will help reignite confidence in the artificial-intelligence trade that has powered the market rally from war-driven lows.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Gold Breaks Below $4,000 as Multi-Year Rally Grinds to a Halt
top_signals.summary: Gold fell below $4,000 an ounce for the first time since November, as the prospect of higher interest rates and a resurgent dollar bring bullion’s three-year bull market to a halt.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: North Korea’s Kim unveils plans for new warships and nuclear-armed navy
top_signals.summary: Kim Jong Un has vowed to modernize his navy, but questions remain whether the investment will ultimately prove worthwhile for the strongman.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: UK banks fear a ‘disaster’ with Ed Miliband as Chancellor
top_signals.summary: City banks are fretting over the prospect of Ed Miliband as Chancellor as Andy Burnham mulls who he will give the keys to Number 11. The banking industry is bracing for a shake-up at the Treasury and fears it could be hit with a fresh raft of taxes under a new Chancellor while key deregulatory [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.
